result.markdown:
---
title: Data Report
---


All information on the data used in the project is compiled in the data report in order to ensure the traceability and reproducibility of the results and to enable a systematic expansion of the database.

The project combines a meteorite-centric dataset with global environmental and economic indicators per country. The raw data is acquired from public sources (and one web scrape), sanitised, and merged into processed datasets that serve as the direct input for the visualization product. The full acquisition and processing pipeline is implemented in `src/` and documented in the project `README.md`; this report describes the resulting datasets.

## Raw data
### Overview Raw Datasets

: Overview of raw datasets used in the project. {#tbl-raw-overview}

| Name | Source | Storage location |
|---|---|---|
| NASA Meteorite Landings | NASA Open Data Portal / The Meteoritical Society – global catalogue of recorded meteorite landings | `data_acquisition/Meteorite_Landings_NASA.csv` |
| Meteorite Prices | Scraped from the online retailer *meteorites-for-sale.com* via `src/helpers/meteorite_price_scraper.py` (Playwright) | `data_acquisition/meteorite_prices.csv` (+ `meteorite_prices_src_urls.txt`) |
| World Bank GDP per Capita 2024 | World Bank – GDP per capita (constant 2015 US\$), indicator `NY.GDP.PCAP.KD` | `data_acquisition/GDP_Per_Capita_2024_worldbank_Data.csv` |
| World Bank Population Density | World Bank – Population density, indicator `EN.POP.DNST` (+ country metadata: region & income group) | `data_acquisition/API_EN.POP.DNST_DS2_en_csv_v2_1453/` |
| CGIAR Global Aridity Index v3 | CGIAR-CSI Global Aridity Index (annual), GeoTIFF raster | `data_acquisition/Global-AridityIndex_v3_annual/ai_v3_yr.tif` |

### Details Dataset 1 – NASA Meteorite Landings

- **Content**: One record per recorded meteorite, with name, classification (`recclass`), mass, whether it *fell* or was *found*, year, and geographic coordinates.
- **Source/provider**: NASA Open Data Portal, originally curated by The Meteoritical Society.
- **Procurement**: Downloaded as a single CSV from the NASA open data portal. No authentication required; a new team member can re-download the file directly and place it at the storage location above.
- **Legal aspects**: Public domain / open data (NASA), free to use.
- **Data governance**: Public, non-personal data.
- **Variable role**: Independent descriptive data; `recclass` and `mass (g)` are later used to derive a predicted price (target of the price-estimation step).

#### Data Catalogue

: Data catalogue for the NASA Meteorite Landings dataset. {#tbl-catalogue-nasa}

| # | Column name | Datatype | Values (range, rules) | Short description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | name | string | non-empty | Meteorite name |
| 2 | id | integer | unique | NASA catalogue identifier |
| 3 | nametype | categorical | {Valid, Relict} | Validity classification |
| 4 | recclass | string | e.g. L5, H4, Iron, IAB-MG | Meteorite classification |
| 5 | mass (g) | float | ≥ 0; may be missing or 0 | Mass in grams |
| 6 | fall | categorical | {Fell, Found} | Observed fall vs. later find |
| 7 | year | integer | may be missing / implausible | Year of fall or find |
| 8 | reclat | float | [-90, 90]; 0 or missing for some | Recorded latitude |
| 9 | reclong | float | [-180, 180]; 0 or missing for some | Recorded longitude |
| 10 | GeoLocation | string | "(lat, long)" | Combined coordinate string |

#### Data Characteristics and Quality

The raw file contains **45 716** records. Known quality issues that motivate the sanitisation step:

- `recclass` values containing the characters `/ ? ( ) ~` (ambiguous / unusable classifications), the literal value `Unknown`, or empty values.
- `nametype = Relict` entries (heavily altered, not representative meteorites).
- Records with missing or `0` mass — these cannot be used for the mass-based price estimation.

### Details Dataset 2 – Meteorite Prices

- **Content**: Catalogue of meteorites offered for sale, with a free-text name and description, an asking price, a retailer category and a mass.
- **Source/provider**: Public product pages of *meteorites-for-sale.com*.
- **Procurement**: Reproducible via the Playwright scraper `src/helpers/meteorite_price_scraper.py` (see `README.md`, Quick Start step 1). The scraper writes a semicolon-separated CSV plus the list of source URLs (`meteorite_prices_src_urls.txt`) for traceability.
- **Legal aspects**: Publicly listed prices, used only to derive an aggregate price model; no redistribution of the underlying catalogue.
- **Data governance**: Public, non-personal data; values are volatile (market prices at scrape time).
- **Variable role**: Source for the category-level price-per-gram model used to estimate meteorite prices.

#### Data Catalogue

: Data catalogue for the Meteorite Prices dataset (raw, `;`-separated). {#tbl-catalogue-prices}

| # | Column name | Datatype | Values (range, rules) | Short description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | name | string | contains a mass token, e.g. "… 21.1g …" | Product / meteorite name |
| 2 | description | string | free text | Product description |
| 3 | price | string | currency-prefixed, e.g. `$26.38` | Asking price |
| 4 | category | string | retailer label, e.g. "Campo del Cielo Iron Meteorite" | Retailer product category |
| 5 | mass | string | gram-suffixed, e.g. `21.1g` | Listed mass |

#### Data Characteristics and Quality

Approximately **249** product records. Quality issues handled in sanitisation: the `price` column carries a `$` prefix and `mass` a `g` suffix (both stored as text, not numeric), and the retailer `category` does not match the NASA `recclass` taxonomy and must be mapped explicitly.

### Details Dataset 3 – World Bank GDP per Capita 2024

- **Content**: GDP per capita (constant 2015 US\$) per country for the year 2024.
- **Source/provider**: World Bank, indicator `NY.GDP.PCAP.KD`.
- **Procurement**: Exported as CSV from the World Bank data portal.
- **Legal aspects**: World Bank open data (CC BY 4.0).
- **Data governance**: Public, non-personal data.

#### Data Catalogue

: Data catalogue for the World Bank GDP dataset. {#tbl-catalogue-gdp}

| # | Column name | Datatype | Values (range, rules) | Short description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Country Name | string | non-empty | Country name |
| 2 | Country Code | string | ISO-3166 alpha-3 | Country code (join key) |
| 3 | Series Name | string | constant | Indicator name |
| 4 | Series Code | string | `NY.GDP.PCAP.KD` | Indicator code |
| 5 | 2024 [YR2024] | float | numeric or `..` for missing | GDP per capita 2024 |

#### Data Characteristics and Quality

Missing values are encoded as `..` and are coerced to numeric / `0` during aggregation. The dataset also contains regional/aggregate rows which are filtered out via the country metadata join.

### Details Dataset 4 – World Bank Population Density

- **Content**: Population density (people per km² of land area) per country and year (1960–2024), plus a country metadata file providing region and income group.
- **Source/provider**: World Bank, indicator `EN.POP.DNST`.
- **Procurement**: World Bank "DS2" CSV bundle (data file + `Metadata_Country` + `Metadata_Indicator`).
- **Legal aspects**: World Bank open data (CC BY 4.0).
- **Data governance**: Public, non-personal data.
- **Note**: The data file has 4 metadata lines before the header (`skiprows=4`); the project uses the `2022` column.

#### Data Catalogue

: Data catalogue for the World Bank Population Density bundle. {#tbl-catalogue-pop}

| # | Column name | Datatype | Values (range, rules) | Short description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Country Name | string | non-empty | Country name |
| 2 | Country Code | string | ISO-3166 alpha-3 | Country code (join key) |
| 3 | Indicator Name / Code | string | `EN.POP.DNST` | Indicator description |
| 4 | 1960 … 2024 | float | yearly columns; many missing (esp. early years) | Population density per year; `2022` used |
| — | Metadata_Country: Region | string | World Bank region; empty for aggregates | Region (used to filter non-countries) |
| — | Metadata_Country: IncomeGroup | categorical | e.g. High / Upper-middle / Lower-middle / Low income | Income group |
| — | Metadata_Country: TableName | string | readable country name | Country display name |

#### Data Characteristics and Quality

Early years contain many missing values; only `2022` is required. Rows whose metadata `Region` is empty are aggregates (e.g. "World", "Euro area") and are excluded so that only actual countries remain.

### Details Dataset 5 – CGIAR Global Aridity Index v3

- **Content**: Global raster of the annual Aridity Index (AI = precipitation / potential evapotranspiration). Low AI = hyper-arid/desert, high AI = humid.
- **Source/provider**: CGIAR-CSI Global Aridity Index v3 (annual). A provider readme PDF is included alongside the raster.
- **Procurement**: Downloaded GeoTIFF (`ai_v3_yr.tif`, ~30 arc-second resolution). Large binary (~0.5 GB).
- **Legal aspects**: CGIAR-CSI data, free for non-commercial / research use with attribution.
- **Data governance**: Public, non-personal geospatial data.
- **Encoding note**: AI values are stored as integers scaled by ×10 000; ocean / no-data pixels are encoded as 0 / nodata.

#### Data Catalogue

: Data catalogue for the Aridity Index raster. {#tbl-catalogue-aridity}

| # | Band / field | Datatype | Values (range, rules) | Short description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Band 1 (raster) | integer | AI × 10 000; nodata for non-land | Annual aridity index, encoded |
| — | derived: lat | float | [-90, 90] | Pixel centre latitude (after downsampling) |
| — | derived: lon | float | [-180, 180] | Pixel centre longitude |
| — | derived: aridity_index | float | ≈ 0.0 (hyper-arid) … > 2 (humid); 0 = ocean/no-data | Decoded aridity index |

#### Data Characteristics and Quality

The raster is far too dense to use directly, so it is downsampled twice: a coarser grid for a per-country mean (reverse-geocoded to country codes) and a finer grid for the pixel-level heatmap. Ocean and no-data pixels decode to exactly `0` and are therefore excluded from the visualization (only `aridity_index > 0` is plotted).

The following figure illustrates the mass distribution of the sanitised meteorite dataset, which motivates the use of a log scale and a median-based price model in the visual encoding:


::: {#cell-fig-mass-dist .cell execution_count=1}
``` {.python .cell-code code-fold="true"}
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

ROOT = Path.cwd()
while not (ROOT / "data_sanitized").exists() and ROOT != ROOT.parent:
    ROOT = ROOT.parent

nasa = pd.read_csv(ROOT / "data_sanitized" / "Meteorite_Landings_NASA_sanitized.csv")
mass = nasa["mass (g)"]
mass = mass[mass > 0]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 3.75))
ax.hist(np.log10(mass), bins=50, color="#8b80d6", edgecolor="white")
ax.set_xlabel("log₁₀(mass in g)")
ax.set_ylabel("Number of meteorites")
ax.set_title("Meteorite mass distribution (sanitised)")
ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display}
![Distribution of meteorite mass (log scale) in the sanitised NASA dataset. The strong right skew motivates a median-based, per-class price-per-gram model.](data_report_files/figure-html/fig-mass-dist-output-1.png){#fig-mass-dist width=566 height=350}
:::
:::


The mass distribution in @fig-mass-dist spans many orders of magnitude, which is why prices are modelled per `recclass` as a robust median price-per-gram rather than via a mean.

## Processed Data
### Overview Processed Datasets

: Overview of processed datasets used in the project. {#tbl-processed-overview}

| Name | Source / produced by | Storage location |
|---|---|---|
| NASA Meteorite Landings (sanitised) | `src/helpers/sanitizers/sanitizer_meteorite_landings.py` | `data_sanitized/Meteorite_Landings_NASA_sanitized.csv` |
| Meteorite Prices (sanitised) | `src/helpers/sanitizers/sanitizer_meteorite_prices.py` | `data_sanitized/meteorite_prices_sanitized.csv` |
| NASA Meteorites with predicted prices | `src/helpers/predict_meteorite_prices.py` | `output_data/Meteorite_Landings_NASA_with_prices.csv` |
| Aggregated country master data | `src/helpers/aggregate_master_from_dataset.py` | `output_data/aggregated_master_data.csv` |
| Aridity pixel grid | `src/helpers/aggregate_master_from_dataset.py` | `output_data/aridity_grid.csv` |

### Details Processed Dataset 1 – NASA Meteorite Landings (sanitised)

- **Content**: The NASA dataset with unusable entries removed (same schema as the raw file, see @tbl-catalogue-nasa).
- **Processing steps & rationale**: Remove rows where `recclass` contains `/ ? ( ) ~`, is `Unknown` or empty; remove `nametype = Relict`; remove rows with missing or `0` mass. This guarantees a usable classification and a positive mass for the price model.
- **Access**: Re-generated via `uv run python src/helpers/sanitizers/sanitizer_meteorite_landings.py` (Quick Start step 2).

#### Data Characteristics and Quality

**43 801** records remain (≈ **1 915** removed). All remaining rows have a valid `recclass` and a strictly positive mass.

### Details Processed Dataset 2 – Meteorite Prices (sanitised)

- **Content**: The scraped prices with numeric `price` and `mass`, and `category` mapped to a valid NASA `recclass` (or `Unknown`).
- **Processing steps & rationale**: Strip the `$` prefix and parse `price` to float; strip the `g` suffix and parse `mass` to float; map each retailer `category` to a valid `recclass` via an explicit lookup so the prices can be joined to the NASA taxonomy. Stays semicolon-separated.
- **Access**: `uv run python src/helpers/sanitizers/sanitizer_meteorite_prices.py`.

#### Data Catalogue

: Data catalogue for the sanitised prices dataset. {#tbl-catalogue-prices-clean}

| # | Column name | Datatype | Values (range, rules) | Short description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | name | string | non-empty | Product / meteorite name |
| 2 | description | string | free text | Product description |
| 3 | price | float | > 0 | Asking price (USD) |
| 4 | category | string | valid `recclass` or `Unknown` | Mapped classification |
| 5 | mass | float | > 0 | Mass in grams |

#### Data Characteristics and Quality

Roughly **249** records. Entries whose category cannot be mapped to a valid `recclass` are set to `Unknown` and are ignored when building the price model.

### Details Processed Dataset 3 – NASA Meteorites with predicted prices

- **Content**: The sanitised NASA dataset plus an added `predicted_price` column.
- **Processing steps & rationale**: Build a per-`recclass` median price-per-gram model from the sanitised prices, then estimate `predicted_price = mass (g) × median price-per-gram`. Entries whose class has no price model match, or with missing/zero mass, receive `0`.
- **Access**: `uv run python src/helpers/predict_meteorite_prices.py` (Quick Start step 3).

#### Data Catalogue

: Additional column relative to @tbl-catalogue-nasa. {#tbl-catalogue-priced}

| # | Column name | Datatype | Values (range, rules) | Short description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 11 | predicted_price | float | ≥ 0; `0` when no class match or no mass | Estimated price in USD |

#### Data Characteristics and Quality

**43 801** records (same rows as Processed Dataset 1). A subset receives `predicted_price = 0` because their `recclass` is absent from the (small) price catalogue — a known limitation driven by the limited size of the scraped price data.

### Details Processed Dataset 4 – Aggregated country master data

- **Content**: One row per country with the merged environmental and economic indicators; the direct input for the country-level choropleth.
- **Processing steps & rationale**: Use the World Bank country metadata as the base (provides name, ISO-3 code, region, income group), filter out non-country aggregates, then left-join population density (2022), GDP per capita (2024) and the per-country mean aridity index (raster reverse-geocoded ISO-2 → ISO-3). Missing values are filled with `0`.
- **Access**: `uv run python src/helpers/aggregate_master_from_dataset.py` (Quick Start step 4).

#### Data Catalogue

: Data catalogue for the aggregated master dataset. {#tbl-catalogue-master}

| # | Column name | Datatype | Values (range, rules) | Short description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | country_name | string | non-empty | Country display name |
| 2 | country_code | string | ISO-3166 alpha-3 | Country code |
| 3 | region | categorical | World Bank region | Region |
| 4 | income_group | categorical | World Bank income group / `Unknown` | Income classification |
| 5 | aridity_index | float | ≥ 0; `0` if unavailable | Mean aridity index |
| 6 | population_density_2022 | float | ≥ 0; `0` if unavailable | People per km² (2022) |
| 7 | gdp_per_capita_2024 | float | ≥ 0; `0` if unavailable | GDP per capita 2024 (USD) |

#### Data Characteristics and Quality

**217** countries. `0` is used as a sentinel for "no data" (e.g. countries absent from a source); this is acceptable for the choropleth because the colour scales are clamped, but it should be read as missing rather than a true zero.

### Details Processed Dataset 5 – Aridity pixel grid

- **Content**: A downsampled pixel grid of the aridity raster for the high-detail heatmap layer.
- **Processing steps & rationale**: The raster is downsampled (~1° grid), AI is decoded (÷10 000), and each valid pixel is exported as a `lat, lon, aridity_index` row.
- **Access**: Produced together with Processed Dataset 4 by `aggregate_master_from_dataset.py`.

#### Data Catalogue

: Data catalogue for the aridity grid. {#tbl-catalogue-grid}

| # | Column name | Datatype | Values (range, rules) | Short description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | lat | float | [-90, 90] | Pixel centre latitude |
| 2 | lon | float | [-180, 180] | Pixel centre longitude |
| 3 | aridity_index | float | `0` = ocean/no-data; > 0 = land | Decoded aridity index |

#### Data Characteristics and Quality

Roughly **93 300** pixels. Ocean and no-data pixels decode to exactly `0`; the visualization plots only `aridity_index > 0` so that the ocean stays clean and the land/water distinction is preserved.
